name: Tartessian (Southwestern)
notes: Tartessian / Southwestern (7th–5th c. BCE). ~95 inscriptions in Southwestern script (Algarve, Alentejo, Huelva). Language affiliation UNKNOWN — Koch (2011) argues Celtic, de Hoz (2010) says non-IE, Untermann links to Iberian. Script partially deciphered; characteristic redundant vowel notation. THIS IS THE MOST SPECULATIVE ENTRY: only the phonological distinctions forced by the script structure are modelled. The voicing distinction in stops is UNRESOLVABLE from the script alone.
